summary: Today’s discourse centered on the persistent challenge of AI hallucination in academic publishing, with Mark Riedl sharing a new reference checker he built to catch fake citations in papers under review—highlighting that PDF-to-text extraction remains an "open problem" and formats vary wildly. While the technical community grappled with research integrity, a lighter note came from Marc Lanctot, whose Bluesky post went viral for celebrating the Tampa Bay Lightning’s improbable playoff elimination by a team of underdogs, including a Costco employee and a rookie goalie. The juxtaposition underscores a day where AI’s reliability issues and the chaos of sports both demanded attention, though the former remains the more pressing trend for developers and researchers.
